GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Italian-Brazilian model Chiara Scelsi on how eyewear can elevate an everyday look", "The model has an eye for combining timeless clothing with an edge, always completing her looks with sunglasses year-round.", "As fashion commits to slowing down, making shopping less about trends and more about lifetime pieces, Chiara Scelsi showcases how accessories elevate her covetable scaled-back style. With timeless items such as tailored shirts, oversized blazers, turtlenecks, or the ever-versatile slip dress, Scelsi cites eyewear as key when it comes to personalising her everyday ensembles. Pictured here in her favourite pair of sunglasses from Tiffany & Co.'s eyewear collection, Scelsi's style melds fun with minimalism, creating an effortless look that can be repeated on morning coffee runs to weekend excursions. When Chiara Scelsi arrived on set in Milan last week, social distancing could not diminish the infectious nature of the 23-year-old and her notorious curly locks. The model, whose debut in fashion turned heads at Paris Fashion Week SS16, exudes glamour and warmth as she explains how comfort comes first in her approach to getting dressed. Formerly a competitive track star, she says statement sunglasses add a personal touch to her favourite, cosiest outfits. "It's important to always feel at ease in what I'm wearing. I travel a lot and I can't have clothes that limit my movement. I love loose fitting clothes, trousers, I love sneakers. And sunglasses are the accessory that complete the look." While Scelsi loves a tracksuit and sunglasses, she also says that her elevated everyday style, on days when she isn't in transit, varies greatly depending on her mood. "My style reflects my extroverted personality...a little wild." For a more formal event, she recommends simplicity as the best route to elegance. "It's about extravagance, without excess, and about mixing different styles together." Citing her Brazilian mom for elements of her style inspiration, she revels in clothing from an old family closet that feels as modern as it did when it was worn by older generations. These pieces find new life on the backs of granddaughters like Scelsi, who add accessories like a handmade beaded necklace or an oversized set of shades. Much like Scelsi's take on fashion, modernity is key for Tiffany & Co.'s approach to creating unique frames. The new eyewear designs reflect Tiffany's legacy of timelessness with subtle sparkling accents of silver, gold and rose gold. Adding mystery to even the boldest extroverts, sunglasses are also important when considering protection from the sun (an essential for those under the glare of Australian days, even in winter) and that added allure is another important factor for Scelsi as she explains her love for a good set. "And, my eyes are very expressive, so when I wear sunglasses others can't immediately understand what I'm thinking!""
